pr.title: Match ef_061D 100% and rename to efspecial (special effects)
pr.author: hourianto

summary: PR #2241 attempted to match the ef_061D special-effects function and rename it to efSync_SpawnSpecial/efspecial. The slice shows broad rewrites in the effect source, header/callsite updates, and symbols.txt renames for the function and local data constants. Review feedback objected to redefining jobj.h inline/assert behavior and the PR was closed with a comment that the code quality did not improve the function, the function was already matched upstream, and the rename should be separated.

key_files.role: Primary effect function rewrite; changed ef_80061D70 toward efSync_SpawnSpecial, replaced EF061D_VA_ARG usage with va_arg, added includes, local data externs/definitions, HSD_ASSERT macro overrides around jobj.h, GET_FIGHTER/FighterBone access, PAD_STACK, and many local variable/name changes.
Symbol rename metadata; ef_80061D70 became efSync_SpawnSpecial and several local data/sdata/sdata2 labels were renamed to ef_* address-style names.
Header touched for the attempted rename/signature surface.
Callsite or integration updates for the renamed special spawn function.
Small adjacent effect-source update; bot report also noted a tiny unmatched-item score change in efAsync_80063930.
Review-comment path for the renamed/new file form; loaded changed-file metadata instead lists src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c, so the exact rename representation is uncertain in this slice.

what_changed: Renamed the function symbol at .text:0x80061D70 from ef_80061D70 to efSync_SpawnSpecial in symbols.txt.

Reworked the main function body from a WIP-style ef_80061D70 implementation using EF061D_VA_ARG and broad locals into a va_arg-based efSync_SpawnSpecial implementation with many named effect, fighter, JObj, float, and stack-pad locals.

Added custom HSD_ASSERT macro override machinery around including baselib/jobj.h, including efSpecial_HSD_ASSERT_MSG and local strings such as "jobj.h", "jobj", and "!(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION)".

Renamed several local data/sdata/sdata2 symbols in symbols.txt from @-style local labels to ef_ address-style names, including ef_803BF9D0, ef_804D39D8, ef_804D39E0, and ef_804D81D0 through ef_804D81F0.
Changed at least one case from raw fighter/user_data offset access to GET_FIGHTER(arg_gobj), fighter_1->parts, and FighterBone joint access.
Touched ef_061D.h, efsync.c, and efasync.c as supporting header/callsite/adjacent effect updates, but the loaded diff excerpt does not show their exact edits.
smart_moves: The PR avoided duplicating inline functions from other modules according to the author, preferring macro/include hackery instead, but reviewers still found that approach questionable.
The rewrite attempted to replace an ad-hoc EF061D_VA_ARG macro and raw vlist_arg temporary with direct va_arg calls, making the argument flow more visible in source.

The case 0x494 rewrite used project fighter structures and GET_FIGHTER/FighterBone access instead of raw user_data pointer arithmetic, which is directionally aligned with typed-field cleanup, though no reviewer acceptance is shown.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A byte/hash-matching claim is not enough if the submitted source depends on brittle macro/include tricks and does not improve the reviewed code quality.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says tooling looked clean and final game file had same hash
human_pr_text: author calls macro things really ugly
issue_comment by ribbanya: PR does not improve code quality and was already matched upstream

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded jobj.h assert/inline behavior should be mapped back to the owning header inline/helper rather than recreated with source-local HSD_ASSERT redefinitions.
evidence_refs: review_comment by PsiLupan: indicative of an inline from jobj.h and should not be necessary to redefine

decomp_lessons.lesson: Rename-only cleanup should be separated from contested function-matching rewrites when review feedback says the function body quality is not improving.
evidence_refs: issue_comment by ribbanya: file can be renamed in a separate PR

decomp_lessons.lesson: Using AI-generated or tooling-generated decomp output requires extra scrutiny for authored-source style; the PR body itself flags brittleness and possible remaining problems.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: mostly done by GPT 5.4 with Opus 4.6
human_pr_text: there might still be problems
issue_comment by ribbanya: does not improve quality

naming_conventions.name: efSync_SpawnSpecial
naming_conventions.observation: Proposed name for ef_80061D70 at 0x80061D70, consistent with nearby efSync_Spawn naming in symbols.txt, but PR evidence shows the PR was not merged and the rename was recommended for a separate PR.

naming_conventions.name: efspecial
naming_conventions.observation: The title and review path indicate an intended rename from ef_061D to efspecial, but changed-file metadata still lists src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c and src/melee/ef/ef_061D.h, so the exact rename state is ambiguous in this slice.

naming_conventions.name: ef_803BF9D0 / ef_804D39D8 / ef_804D81D0-style locals
naming_conventions.observation: The PR renamed @-style local labels to address-style ef_* names for strings/floats/doubles used by the rewritten function. Review acceptance for these data names is not shown.

naming_conventions.name: arg_gobj, ret_obj, eff_1, eff_2, fighter_1, fighter_bone_1, jobj_1, jobj_2
naming_conventions.observation: The rewrite introduced role-ish local names for objects and pointers, but several remain numbered/generic, suggesting a matching-oriented rather than fully semantic naming pass.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(0x98) and explicit stack padding locals sp_pad128/sp_pad130 to steer stack layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Loaded evidence does not include objdiff/checkdiff proof for this tactic; global standards require narrow verifier evidence for PAD_STACK and local stack-shape tactics.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Pulled float/double constants and assert strings into named local data symbols to match references/order.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This crosses into data/symbol ownership; loaded review evidence does not show acceptance, and standards warn against naming literals/data solely for matching.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Redefined HSD_ASSERT around including jobj.h to control header inline assert file/message behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Reviewer explicitly objected that this indicated a jobj.h inline and should not require redefining.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed va_list handling from custom EF061D_VA_ARG macro and vlist_arg temporary to direct va_arg(vlist, type) calls, including void* for some effect factory calls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: EF061D_VA_ARG deletion and va_arg additions
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: No loaded review confirms the final va_arg shapes are accepted; author described the related areas as brittle.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used typed fighter access in one shown branch: GET_FIGHTER(arg_gobj), fighter_1->parts, and FighterBone joint fields instead of raw offsets from user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Directionally cleaner, but this PR was closed for not improving function quality overall, so do not treat it as accepted for this function.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: The custom assertion/inline pattern was indicative of an inline from jobj.h and should not need to be redefined.

review_feedback.author: hourianto
review_feedback.feedback: Author clarified that their earlier concern was about macro hackery with defines/includes at the top of the file and said the in-function inline areas were brittle but they would see if using the inline was possible.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The PR did not improve code quality for the function, the function was already matched upstream, and any file rename should be done separately.

review_feedback.author: unknown reviewer in metadata excerpt
review_feedback.feedback: A todo comment was added and the reviewer said the issue did not seem straightforward.

follow_up_queries: PR 2241 efSync_SpawnSpecial jobj.h inline HSD_ASSERT redefine

notes: Loaded diff and review comments are truncated, so some exact header/callsite edits and the second review-comment context are not fully visible.
The author reported clean tooling and a final game-file hash match, but no local objdiff/checkdiff/build log is loaded beyond a bot report showing two tiny unmatched-item improvements.
PR state is closed with merged_at null; do not treat the attempted match, rename, data-symbol names, or macro strategy as accepted.
There is path ambiguity: review comments refer to src/melee/ef/efspecial.c while changed-file metadata lists src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c and src/melee/ef/ef_061D.h.
Reviewer feedback gives strong evidence against the jobj.h assert redefinition tactic and against bundling the contested rewrite with the rename.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not replace identifiable jobj.h inlines with local HSD_ASSERT redefinition machinery
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2241, a reviewer identified the custom HSD_ASSERT/jobj.h include hack as evidence of a jobj.h inline and said it should not be necessary to redefine it. This supports preferring existing header inline/helper forms over source-local macro overrides.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Separate rename-only work from contested matching rewrites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2241 was closed with feedback that the function body did not improve quality and was already matched upstream, while the file rename could happen in a separate PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Hash or tooling cleanliness claims still need reviewable authored-source quality
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR body claimed the final game file had the same hash and tooling looked clean, but the author also called the macro approach ugly and reviewers rejected the quality of the rewrite.

source_update_candidates.title: AI-generated matching output needs normal authored-source and review-quality scrutiny
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an explicit note that AI-generated or tool-generated decomp rewrites are not exempt from authored-source style, header-inline, verification, and review-quality expectations. PR #2241 is evidence where the author disclosed AI-generated work, macro hackery, and brittleness, and the PR was closed for not improving code quality despite a hash/tooling claim.
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards already cover authored-source style and verification, but the PR provides a concrete historical example for an AI/tooling-specific note if the curator wants one.

source_update_candidates.title: ef_061D/efspecial rename ambiguity in PR #2241
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2241 intended to rename ef_061D to efspecial and used efSync_SpawnSpecial for the 0x80061D70 function, but the PR was closed unmerged and reviewer feedback said the file rename should happen in a separate PR. Do not infer accepted path/name ownership from this PR alone.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future search distinguish an attempted unmerged rename from accepted path facts.

rejection_notes: Do not add efSync_SpawnSpecial as an accepted canonical function name from this PR alone; it was an unmerged symbols.txt rename and reviewer feedback recommended separating rename work.
Do not accept ef_803BF9D0/ef_804D39D8/ef_804D81D0-style data names as source-owned facts from this PR alone; the loaded evidence shows rename attempts but no acceptance.
Do not treat the custom efSpecial_HSD_ASSERT_MSG/HSD_ASSERT override pattern as a recommended matching tactic; it received direct reviewer pushback.
Do not record the claimed 100% match or final hash as verified by intake; the loaded evidence only includes the author claim and a small bot report, not full objdiff/checkdiff validation.
